perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, both parties, for their mutual benefit, desire to have the State disclose to Vendor certain specifications, designs, plans, drawings or other business and/or technical information which is proprietary, restricted or secret, and confidential to the State (“CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION").
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, both parties, for their mutual benefit, desire to have the State disclose to Vendor certain specifications, designs, plans, drawings or other business and/or technical information which is proprietary, restricted or secret, and sensitive to the State (“CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential' to 'sensitive' introduces a contradiction. While 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' is defined, 'sensitive information' is not. This change suggests that the information disclosed is merely sensitive, potentially weakening the agreement's protections. This undermines the overall intention of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to safeguard confidentiality. This is particularly conflicting with the later sections that explicitly refer to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor shall use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION only for the purpose of discussing street level route information for the State’s fiber optic network data points and paths with the State’s support team in discussions regarding the State’s fiber project. Vendor shall restrict disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees with a need to know (and advise such employees of the obligations assumed herein), and shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party without prior written approval of the State. These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential' to 'sensitive' weakens the protection of the disclosed information. The original text defines 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'. The changed text uses 'sensitive', which is undefined. The contradicted text relies on the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to restrict its use and disclosure. By changing the initial definition, the later restrictions become unclear, as 'sensitive information' is not subject to the same contractual protections as 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', which is an in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: NOW, THEREFORE, the State and Vendor agree as follows: 1. Vendor shall use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION only for the purpose of discussing street level route information for the State’s fiber optic network data points and paths with the State’s support team in discussions regarding the State’s fiber project. Vendor shall restrict disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees with a need to know (and advise such employees of the obligations assumed herein), and shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party without prior written approval of the State.
perturbation.changed_text: NOW, THEREFORE, the State and Vendor agree as follows: 1. Vendor shall use such PROTECTED DATA only for the purpose of discussing street level route information for the State’s fiber optic network data points and paths with the State’s support team in discussions regarding the State’s fiber project. Vendor shall restrict disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees with a need to know (and advise such employees of the obligations assumed herein), and shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party without prior written approval of the State.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' with 'PROTECTED DATA' at the beginning of section 1 introduces ambiguity because 'PROTECTED DATA' is not defined in the agreement. However, the rest of the section still uses 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.' This creates a conflict regarding which term actually governs the Vendor's usage and disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor shall use such PROTECTED DATA only for the purpose of discussing street level route information for the State’s fiber optic network data points and paths with the State’s support team in discussions regarding the State’s fiber project. Vendor shall restrict disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees with a need to know (and advise such employees of the obligations assumed herein), and shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party without prior written approval of the State. These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' with 'PROTECTED DATA' in the first instance within section 1, but the rest of the section still refers to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.' This introduces misaligned terminology as described in the Perturbation Explanation because 'PROTECTED DATA' is not defined in the agreement, creating confusion as to which term governs the vendor's usage and disclosure obligations. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text and uses both terms, which strengthens the argument that a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any DATA: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to 'DATA' introduces a contradiction, as 'DATA' is an extremely broad term that is not defined, while the rest of the subsection still refers to the originally defined 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'. This creates a significant ambiguity about what is actually exempt from the disclosure restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any DATA: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' with 'DATA'. The explanation states that 'DATA' is an undefined term while the rest of the section refers to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'. Since the contradicted text is identical to the changed text and the explanation states this change leads to a significant ambiguity about what is actually exempt from the disclosure restrictions. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, creating an in-text contradiction.
